title: ‘Kicked in abdomen repeatedly’: UP man booked for killing unborn children over dowry demands
description: In Bhadohi, a woman has accused her husband and two in-laws of causing the death of her unborn children through dowry-related assaults during pregnancies. The complaint details repeated physical abuse, including kicks to the abdomen, leading to two miscarriages. Authorities have registered a case under relevant sections of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and the Dowry Prohibition Act.

description: Yann LeCun, Meta's former chief AI scientist and deep learning pioneer, has dismissed the panic around Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview as "BS from self-delusion." description: Melanie Collins closed her eighth NFL season with CBS after covering the NFC Championship Game, continuing her steady rise as a trusted sideline reporter. Working alongside Kevin Harlan and Trent Green, she has become a consistent presence in high-profile broadcasts. With a background that includes ESPN, Yahoo Sports, and major global events, Collins brings depth and versatility to her role. As the offseason begins, she is taking time away before an expected return for the next NFL season.

‘Urdu-speaking’ assembly seats in UP record lower voter deletions
description: Urdu-speaking Assembly constituencies in Uttar Pradesh, many with significant Muslim populations, have shown lower voter deletion rates and slightly higher addition rates compared to the state average. These 46 constituencies saw 26.33 lakh deletions and added 11.79 lakh voters during the recent revision period.

title: France says no to Microsoft Windows and the reason is America
description: France is ditching Microsoft Windows for Linux, a significant step in Europe's push for digital sovereignty and reduced reliance on American tech giants. This move, aimed at regaining control over data and digital infrastructure, aligns with broader EU efforts to lessen dependence on foreign technology. The initiative begins with government digital agencies, signaling a strategic shift in national digital policy.

title: Quote of the day: Desire beyond this world points to another, says CS Lewis
description: C.S. Lewis, a renowned 20th-century writer, shaped literature and theology. Born in Ireland, his life journey included loss, intellectual growth, and a profound return to Christianity. His clear, engaging style made complex ideas accessible. Lewis authored influential Christian apologetics and the beloved fantasy series 'The Chronicles of Narnia'. His impactful radio talks and personal life also left a lasting mark.

title: Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry make noble move, continue giving back
description: Stephen Curry, the NBA legend, sparked shockwaves when he ended his decade long partnership with Under Armour in 2025, after over 10 years of being with the brand exclusively. While Stephen Curry remained vague about the reason behind it, it seems there was a pretty noble thought that led to this decision and his wife, Ayesha Curry, has been a supporter through all of it.
